behaviors.description: The keyword-frequency analyzer assigns the correct sentiment when the review contains only words from the configured positive and negative word lists.
behaviors.justification: This is deterministic scoring logic at a single analyzer boundary. Real failures here are threshold/counting bugs that are reproducible via direct function inputs; mocks are not required and would not add failure-detection power.
behaviors.test_description: Call the keyword-frequency analyzer directly with table-driven inputs (positive-only, negative-only, neutral counts) and explicit threshold config; assert exact sentiment label outputs and boundary conditions.

behaviors.description: The explanation analyzer produces reasoning that references specific phrases from the original review, not generic boilerplate.
behaviors.justification: If the explanation component is model-driven natural-language reasoning, correctness depends on real model output quality/variability. A mock can easily hide generic-output failures by returning ideal text, so real model invocation is needed.
behaviors.test_description: Run the explanation analyzer against a fixed corpus of reviews using the real model/API; assert each explanation includes at least one concrete phrase span from the input review and fails when output is generic boilerplate. Execute multiple runs to detect instability/regressions in grounding behavior.

behaviors.description: When a review contains mixed signals (both positive and negative keywords), the sentiment score reflects the configured tie-breaking rule.
behaviors.justification: Tie-breaking is deterministic policy logic inside the keyword analyzer. Failures are pure logic/config interpretation bugs reproducible without I/O or external dependencies.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke analyzer function with mixed positive/negative keyword counts under multiple tie-rule configurations (e.g., prefer negative, prefer neutral, threshold-first) and assert exact expected sentiment for each config.

behaviors.description: The explanation for a sarcastic review (e.g., "Great, another broken feature") correctly identifies the negative intent despite positive surface words.
behaviors.justification: Sarcasm intent detection is semantic/model-quality behavior, not deterministic string matching. Mocked analyzers can mask this failure; real model behavior is required to catch misclassification of intent.
behaviors.test_description: Use a sarcasm-focused evaluation set with real explanation generation; assert explanations explicitly capture negative intent (not literal positive wording) using rubric-based checks or model-graded criteria. Run repeated trials to measure consistency.

behaviors.description: Both outputs (score and explanation) are returned together in a single response, with the explanation referencing the same sentiment direction as the score.
behaviors.justification: This is partly orchestration, but real failure risk includes divergence between deterministic score and model-generated explanation direction. Pure mocks can hide this by returning aligned outputs; real explanation generation is needed to detect true mismatch behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Call the full service endpoint with real explanation generation enabled; assert response contains both fields in one payload and that explanation sentiment direction matches the score label across a mixed test set. Include repeated runs for borderline inputs to catch nondeterministic mismatches.
